compiled_state.title: SC6 gap: integration test does not invoke validate.sh --ci on a template-installed project
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Success criterion 6 of epic d000-2598 requires validate.sh --ci to pass on a project created via the template onboarding path. The existing test (test-template-validate-integration.sh, 40 assertions) covers the registry-to-detection pipeline and config format checks but does not actually invoke validate.sh --ci on a template-installed project.

## Why This Is Hard

Running validate.sh --ci on a template-installed project requires actually installing a template (nava-platform from GitHub), which is a network-dependent operation not suitable for pure unit/integration tests. This is essentially an E2E test that needs CI infrastructure.

## Proposed Fix

Create a CI-only E2E test that:
1. Installs nava-platform via uv in a temp directory
2. Runs nava-platform app install for at least one template (NextJS)
3. Runs validate.sh --ci on the result
4. Asserts exit 0

This should run in GitHub Actions (not local test suite) since it requires network access and a clean environment.

## Done Definition
- CI workflow exists that runs validate.sh --ci on a template-installed project
- Workflow passes for at least NextJS template

comments.body: Escalated to user: SC6 requires CI infrastructure (template installation from GitHub). Cannot be resolved in unit test scope. Tracking as standalone follow-up.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
